reason_for_decision: The article passes because it compares fixed and mobile systems through power, backhaul, calibration, and command workflow instead of through superficial deployment labels.
strengths: The article is particularly good on how setup time, calibration, and local constraints change the value of mobile systems.
It treats fixed systems as infrastructure commitments rather than default choices, which improves the comparison.
The hybrid-model guidance is realistic for operators with both persistent and temporary mission needs.
summary_assessment: This article passes comfortably. It is practical, balanced, and useful for teams deciding between permanent and deployable surveillance models.
